name.example: Old Toronto, Toronto, Golden Horseshoe, Ontario, Canada

location.description: ID of a pre-defined location, GeoJSON geometry or latlong array - see [formats](https://docs.tomorrow.io/reference/api-formats#locations)

fields.description: Selected fields from our data layers (polygon/polyline default to Max, if not suffix is not specified)

units.description: Unit system of the field values, either "metric" or "imperial" - see [Field Descriptors](https://docs.tomorrow.io/reference/weather-data-layers#field-descriptors)

timesteps.description: Timesteps of the timelines: "1m", "5m", "15m", "30m", "1h", "1d" or "current" (defaults to "1h") - see [Timestep Availability](https://docs.tomorrow.io/reference/weather-data-layers#timestep-availability)

startTime.description: Start time can be "now", "nowPlus`X`m/h/d", "nowMinus`X`m/h/d" or ISO 8601 format "2019-03-20T14:09:50Z". (defaults to now)

endTime.description: End time can be "now", "nowPlus`X`m/h/d", "nowMinus`X`m/h/d" or ISO 8601 format "2019-03-20T14:09:50Z". (defaults to now)

timezone.description: The timezone parameter displays the response datetime in the requested timezone. The timezone parameter is not affecting the request startTime and endTime. If you like to request data at a specific timezone, please add the timezone (in ISO 8601 format) to the startTime and endTime parameters. Timezone of time values, according to IANA Timezone Names (defaults to UTC) - see [Formats](https://docs.tomorrow.io/reference/api-formats#timezone). Using the `auto` value will automatically set the timezone of the request location

dailyStartHour.description: This setting is used only with daily timesteps (1d) and determines when the daily aggregation period begins. By default, the aggregation runs from 6 AM to 6 AM. You can adjust this by setting dailyStartHour to any value between 0 and 23 (where 0 represents midnight, 1 represents 1 AM, and so on). For example, to aggregate from midnight to 11:59 PM, set dailyStartHour to 0.

parameters.name: location
parameters.description: ID of a pre-defined location or latlong string - [lat, lon] for additional formats, see [Formats](https://docs.tomorrow.io/reference/api-formats#locations)

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: Selected fields from our data layers (polygon defaults to Max, if not suffix is not specified)

parameters.name: units
parameters.description: Unit system of the field values, either "metric" or "imperial" - see [Field Descriptors](https://docs.tomorrow.io/reference/data-layers-overview#field-descriptors)

parameters.name: timesteps
parameters.description: Timesteps of the timelines: "best", "1m", "5m", "15m", "30m", "1h", "1d" or "current" (defaults to 1h) - see [Timestep Availability](https://docs.tomorrow.io/reference/data-layers-overview#timestep-availability)

parameters.name: startTime
parameters.description: Start time can be "now", "nowPlus`X`m/h/d", "nowMinus`X`m/h/d" or ISO 8601 format "2019-03-20T14:09:50Z". (defaults to now)

parameters.name: endTime
parameters.description: End time can be "now", "nowPlus`X`m/h/d", "nowMinus`X`m/h/d" or ISO 8601 format "2019-03-20T14:09:50Z". (defaults to now)

parameters.name: timezone
parameters.description: The timezone parameter displays the response datetime in the requested timezone. The timezone parameter is not affecting the request startTime and endTime. If you like to request data at a specific timezone, please add the timezone (in ISO 8601 format) to the startTime and endTime parameters. Timezone of time values, according to IANA Timezone Names (defaults to UTC) - see [Formats](https://docs.tomorrow.io/reference/api-formats#timezone). Using the `auto` value will automatically set the timezone of the request location

parameters.name: legs
parameters.description: Collection of route segments, each represented as a pair of location object and a duration (in minutes)

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: Selected fields from our data layers (polygon/polyline default to Max, if no suffix is specified)

parameters.name: startTime
parameters.description: Start time in ISO 8601 format "2019-03-20T14:09:50Z" (defaults to now)

parameters.name: units
parameters.description: Unit system of the field values, either "metric" or "imperial" - see [Field Descriptors](https://docs.tomorrow.io/reference/weather-data-layers#field-descriptors)

parameters.name: timezone
parameters.description: Timezone of time values, according to IANA Timezone Names (defaults to UTC) - see [Formats](https://docs.tomorrow.io/reference/api-formats#timezone)

parameters.name: zoom
parameters.description: The zoom level for the map, 1-12

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Specifies the format of the returned tiles (defaults to .png)

parameters.name: gradient
parameters.description: Defines custom color gradient for the map. i.e "1:93e4dd4d, 2:94d6ab4d, 3:9bca864d". The key represents the position as an integer and the value is the color code in HEX format.

Result.value: // 	The map image in requested format

name.example: Austin, Travis County, Texas, United States

location.description: ID of a pre-defined location, GeoJSON geometry or lat long array - see [formats](https://docs.tomorrow.io/reference/api-formats#locations)

fields.description: Selected fields from our data layers (polygon/polyline default to Max, if not suffix is not specified) - see [Historical Data Layers](https://docs.tomorrow.io/reference/historical)

timesteps.description: Timesteps of the historical: "1h" or "1d" (defaults to "1h"). "1d" timesteps will default select the Max value of the fields. Only 1 timestep can be requested in each API call

startTime.description: Start time can be "nowMinus`X`m/h/d" or ISO 8601 format "2019-03-20T14:09:50Z".

endTime.description: End time can be "nowMinus`X`m/h/d" or ISO 8601 format "2019-03-20T14:09:50Z".

units.description: Unit system of the field values, either "metric" or "imperial" - see [Field Descriptors](https://docs.tomorrow.io/reference/weather-data-layers#field-descriptors)

timezone.description: The timezone parameter displays the response datetime in the requested timezone. The timezone parameter is not affecting the request startTime and endTime. If you like to request data at a specific timezone, please add the timezone (in ISO 8601 format) to the startTime and endTime parameters. Timezone of time values, according to IANA Timezone Names (defaults to UTC) - see [Formats](https://docs.tomorrow.io/reference/api-formats#timezone)

location.description: ID of a pre-defined location, GeoJSON geometry or lat long array - see [formats](https://docs.tomorrow.io/reference/api-formats#locations)

fields.description: Selected fields from our data layers (polygon/polyline default to Max, if not suffix is not specified) - see [Historical Data Layers](https://docs.tomorrow.io/reference/historical)

startDate.description: Climate normals only support "MM-DD" datetime format and is limited to 365 days. The climate normals aggregates data from 2000 to 2020.

endDate.description: Climate normals only support "MM-DD" datetime format and is limited to 365 days. The climate normals aggregates data from 2000 to 2020.

units.description: Unit system of the field values, either "metric" or "imperial" - see [Field Descriptors](https://docs.tomorrow.io/reference/weather-data-layers#field-descriptors)

geometry.description: The GeoJSON geometry representation of the location object

direction.description: The location direction in degrees clockwise from due north

name.description: The name of the location. Name or direction parameters must be specified

direction.description: The location direction in degrees clockwise from due north

tags.description: List of tags to be added to locations
List of tags to be removed from locations

name.description: The name of the insight used when setting alert notifications

rules.description: The [rules](https://docs.tomorrow.io/reference/insights-overview#rules-language) for which the linked locations will be checked. **Either rules or conditions is required** i.e (windSpeed > 30)

conditions.description: The [conditions](https://docs.tomorrow.io/reference/insights-overview#conditions-language-ast-trees) for which the linked locations will be checked. Either rules or conditions is required

tags.description: Any descriptive tags to be used to filter insights

severity.description: The code denoting the intensity of impact when conditions occur (extreme, severe, moderate, minor, unknown)

description.description: The description detailing this insight use-case

name.description: The name of the insight used when triggering alert notifications

rules.description: The [rules](https://docs.tomorrow.io/reference/insights-overview#rules-language) for which the linked locations will be checked. i.e (windSpeed > 30)

conditions.description: The [conditions](https://docs.tomorrow.io/reference/insights-overview#conditions-language-ast-trees) for which the linked locations will be checked.

tags.description: Any descriptive tags to be used to filter insights

severity.description: The code denoting the intensity of impact when conditions occur (extreme, severe, moderate, minor, unknown)

description.description: The description detailing this insight use-case

tags.description: List of tags to be added to insights
List of tags to be removed from insights

name.description: The name of the alert used by triggered alerts

insight.description: ID of a custom insight or pre-defined Severe Weather Event category - e.g. "floods" or ”lightning” for lightning alerts

isActive.description: Defines whether the alert triggers notifications to the webhook

notifications.description: The configuration of the notifications sent to the webhook (`PRIOR`, `START`, and `END` are available for Insight Alerts and `PUBLISH` is available for Severe Weather Event Alerts only).

lightningConfig.description: configuration of lightning alert with following properties:  `lightningTypes` - an array of possible lightning types based on which alert detection happens. Allowed values: `C2C` (cloud to cloud) and `C2G` (cloud to ground). `buffers` - an array of radiuses based on which the alert detection happens (as soon as lightning strike occurs in one of the buffer, the alert notification is triggered). Minimum buffer value 0.6 and maximum value 48. Note that only first strike within the buffer triggers the alert notification.  `ttl` - duration of all-clear notification since the last strike happen within the buffer `distanceUnit` - the unit of buffer distance, allowed values `km` (kilometers) or `mi` (miles)

name.description: The name of the alert used by triggered alerts

notifications.description: The configuration of the notifications sent to the webhook (`PRIOR`, `START`, and `END` are available for Insight Alerts and `PUBLISH` is available for Severe Weather Event Alerts only).

lightningConfig.description: configuration of lightning alert with following properties:  `lightningTypes` - an array of possible lightning types based on which alert detection happens. Allowed values: `C2C` (cloud to cloud) and `C2G` (cloud to ground). `buffers` - an array of radiuses based on which the alert detection happens (as soon as lightning strike occurs in one of the buffer, the alert notification is triggered). Minimum buffer value 0.6 and maximum value 48. Note that only first strike within the buffer triggers the alert notification.  `ttl` - duration of all-clear notification since the last strike happen within the buffer `distanceUnit` - the unit of buffer distance, allowed values `km` (kilometers) or `mi` (miles)

locations.description: The list of Locations by ID to be linked to this Alert.
The list of Point locations by ID to be unlinked from this alert.

parameters.name: location
parameters.description: ID of a pre-defined location or latlong string - see [formats](https://docs.tomorrow.io/reference/api-formats#locations)

parameters.name: insights
parameters.description: Pre-defined category names or custom insight IDs

parameters.name: buffer
parameters.description: The buffer around locations, in case of pre-defined insight categories (in km)

location.description: ID of a pre-defined location, GeoJSON geometry or latlong array - see [formats](https://docs.tomorrow.io/reference/api-formats#locations)

insights.description: Pre-defined category names or custom insight IDs

buffer.description: The buffer around locations, in case of pre-defined insight categories (in km)

parameters.name: feedName
parameters.description: The nama of your custom feed

timeRange.description: Duration to get the feed results for. Times should follow the ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ)

geofence.description: Fields: geojsonGeometry (object; required) – Valid GeoJSON geometry (e.g., Polygon or MultiPolygon). type (string; required) – The GeoJSON geometry type (e.g., "Polygon"). coordinates (array; required) – The coordinates defining the polygon.

attributes.description: insightId (string) – The unique ID of the triggered insight. insightName (string) – The name of the insight. locationId (string) – An identifier for the location category. severity (string) – The severity of the event. insightDescription (string) – The textual description of the insight.

insight.description: This is the conditions to be met. You can either pass here [Rules](https://docs.tomorrow.io/reference/insights-overview#rules-language-recommended) i.e {"rules": "(temperature > 30)"}. [conditions](https://docs.tomorrow.io/reference/insights-overview#conditions-language-ast-trees) i.e {"conditions": "...") or InsightId, i.e. {"insightId": "...."}

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: If left blank, the official government TAF will be used. If the source is set to “model”, a TAF will be generated using Tomorrow.io weather data.

route.description: The route to evaluate, containing time window and legs.

start.description: Route start time in ISO‑8601 format.

end.description: Route end time in ISO‑8601 format.

locations.description: One or more geometries representing the leg path/area.

legs.description:
Ordered list of route segments. Up to 1000 segments are supported.
A RouteLocation is valid when geojsonGeometry is provided. Each RouteLeg must include at least one RouteLocation.

originTrigger.description:
Trigger conditions evaluated at the route origin. A Trigger must include either insightId or conditions.
At least one of originTrigger, destinationTrigger, or routeTrigger is required.

conditions.description:
Tree of conditions to be met- [conditions](https://docs.tomorrow.io/reference/insights-overview#conditions-language-ast-trees) i.e {"conditions": "..."). 
Up to 6 nested conditions levels are supported.

destinationTrigger.description: Trigger conditions evaluated at the route destination. A Trigger must include either insightId or conditions.

conditions.description:
Tree of conditions to be met- [conditions](https://docs.tomorrow.io/reference/insights-overview#conditions-language-ast-trees) i.e {"conditions": "..."). 
Up to 6 nested conditions levels are supported.

routeTrigger.description: Trigger conditions applied across the entire route/legs. A Trigger must include either insightId or conditions.

conditions.description:
Tree of conditions to be met- [conditions](https://docs.tomorrow.io/reference/insights-overview#conditions-language-ast-trees) i.e {"conditions": "..."). 
Up to 6 nested conditions levels are supported.

parameters.name: zoom
parameters.description: The zoom level for the map, 1-12

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Specifies the format of the returned tiles (defaults to .png)

parameters.name: gradient
parameters.description: Defines custom color gradient for the map. i.e "1:93e4dd4d, 2:94d6ab4d, 3:9bca864d". The key represents the position as an integer and the value is the color code in HEX format.

Result.value: // 	The map image in requested format

parameters.name: location
parameters.description: ID of a pre-defined location or latlong string - see [formats](https://docs.tomorrow.io/reference/api-formats#locations)

parameters.name: insights
parameters.description: Pre-defined category names or custom insight IDs

parameters.name: buffer
parameters.description: The buffer around locations, in case of pre-defined insight categories (in km)
